texto: I also noticed that as you go to sleep the ideas continue, but they become less and less logically interconnected. You don't notice that they're not logically connected until you ask yourself, "What made me think of that?" and you try to work your way back, and often you can't remember what the hell did make you think of that!
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Ralph Leighton que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “I also noticed that as you go to sleep the ideas continue, but they become less and less logicall…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, sleeping, thinking, com foco em trabalho e thinking.

frase_original: I also noticed that as you go to sleep the ideas continue, but they become less and less logically interconnected. You don't notice that they're not logically connected until you ask yourself, "What made me think of that?" and you try to work your way back, and often you can't remember what the hell did make you think of that!

texto: Once I get on a puzzle, I can't get off. If my mother's friend had said, "Never mind, it's too much work," I'd have blown my top, because I want to beat this damn thing, as long as I've gone this far. I can't just leave it after I've found out so much about it. I have to keep going to find out ultimately what is the matter with it in the end.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Ralph Leighton sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “Once I get on a puzzle, I can't get off. If my mother's friend had said, "Never mind, it's too mu…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a motivacao, motivation, reflexao, útil em momentos de termino e trabalho.
